what_it_does: TypeScript declaration file for the Avatar component. Declares an AvatarProps interface extending React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement> with optional src, alt, gold (filled gold variant), and size (number px or any CSS length), and declares the Avatar function returning JSX.Element, exported as both named and default.
observations: AvatarProps extends React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement> (the avatar renders as a span)
Props: src?, alt?, gold? (filled gold variant), size? (number|string)
JSDoc: 'Circular avatar — a photo (src) or initials/children. gold = filled gold variant.'
Both named export (Avatar) and default export declared
standouts: size typed as number | string with the px-vs-CSS-length intent documented inline
conventions: Co-located .d.ts beside the .jsx, mirroring its surface — a typing convention for the component set
Extends the native element attribute type so all standard span attributes pass through
notes: The type contract for Avatar: a span-based circular avatar taking src/alt/gold/size; mirrors the Avatar.jsx implementation.
purpose_vs_actual: Match — it is a declaration file and it accurately declares the Avatar component's public type surface consistent with Avatar.jsx.
apparent_themes: Brand 'gold' variant as a first-class prop
intention_signals: To give consumers a precise, IDE-visible type contract for Avatar while keeping it a thin token-class wrapper over a span.
contribution: Contributes the typed public interface for the Avatar component (prop names, types, the gold variant flag, the number|string size convention) that a fused system would keep as Avatar's contract.
summary_for_embedding: TypeScript declarations for the Avatar component: an AvatarProps interface (src, alt, gold, size) extending span HTML attributes, with named and default exports.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a precise type contract for the circular Avatar wrapper so consumers get typed props including the gold variant and flexible size.
